items.title: PM rejects claims linking him to funds allegedly kept in Africa
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim rejected allegations linking him to overseas fund ownership, saying they must be backed by strong evidence. Commenting on claims linked to a viral video involving an individual known as Albert Tay, Anwar said he would not entertain any baseless allegations or accusations. Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz had called on the authorities to investigate a viral recording containing serious allegations made by an individual known as Sofia Rini involving alleged misconduct linked to a former Prime Minister's officer.

items.title: Govt to consider restricting ministerial travel amid global supply crisis, says Anwar
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — The government is refining stricter measures to reduce energy consumption amid pressure from the global supply crisis, including considering limiting the movement of Cabinet members and ministries, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “We want to avoid (supply shortage issues). Elaborating, the Prime Minister said MTEN now meets every two days with ministries and relevant parties to monitor the overall energy supply situation.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia negotiating with Iran over stranded tankers
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Malaysia is in talks with Iran to ensure two Malaysian-owned tankers carrying fuel supplies are allowed to proceed after exiting the Strait of Hormuz, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. "One vessel has already arrived (in Pengerang, Johor), while another is close and entering Malaysian waters. However, two vessels that have already exited (the Strait of Hormuz) are still facing several restrictions.

items.title: MetMalaysia: Heavy rain, thunderstorms to hit 10 states until 7pm today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit 10 states until 7pm today, according to Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). The warning also applies to Penang (Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan) and Perak (Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang and Muallim). Other states involved are Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera, Pekan and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang), and Negeri Sembilan (Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, Jempol and Tampin).

items.title: Johor police arrest suspected drug ring mastermind and girlfriend, seize RM1.95m in contraband
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Johor police have crippled a drug syndicate believed active since early this year after raiding an apartment in Aliff Heights here and seizing drugs and assets worth RM1.95 million. "Upon inspection, police found a large quantity of drugs, including over 11.6 kilogrammes of MDMA, 13 packets of ecstasy powder (0.342kg), one packet of ketamine (0.02kg), one packet of syabu (0.11kg), 1,796 Erimin 5 pills, along with repackaging equipment. Ab Rahaman said police also seized two cars and cash worth RM146,710, bringing the total value of drugs seized and assets confiscated to RM1.95 million.

items.title: Man arrested over fatal stabbing of ex-wife in Setapak
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have detained a man linked to the fatal stabbing of his ex-wife at a condominium in Setapak yesterday. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the incident occurred at Genting Court Condominium at about 2.20pm on April 23, after police received a distress call from the public. Fadil urged those with information to contact the Wangsa Maju district police headquarters at 03-9289 9222, the Kuala Lumpur police hotline at 03-2115 9999, or investigating officer assistant superintendent of police Baldev Singh at 012-480 6062, or to visit the nearest police station.

items.title: Gobind says MSME digital grant payment delays to be resolved by end-April
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 24 — Issues involving delayed disbursement of funds under the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Digital Grant (MSME) Madani are expected to be resolved by the end of this month, Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said today. He said the matter was raised at a recent National Economic Action Council meeting chaired by Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim. The MSME Digital Grant Madani offers matching grants of up to 50 per cent, capped at RM5,000, for Malaysian-owned small and medium enterprises to adopt digital tools such as e-invoicing, point-of-sale systems and marketing tools, with applications managed through Bank Simpanan Nasional.

items.title: Govt considering monthly allowance for Form 6 students - PM
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The government is considering extending monthly allowances to Form Six students, similar to those currently provided to students in the Matriculation Programme, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. "I agree that allowances for Form Six and matriculation students should be equalised. Anwar was responding to a question from a Form Six student on why Form Six students do not currently receive allowances like matriculation students, despite both being at the pre-university level.

items.title: Retiree in fear as sinkhole appears on his property due to drainage failure
items.summary: KUANTAN: A broken drainage culvert in Sungai Karang Pantai, here, has become not just a nuisance for a retiree; it has now become a growing worry. This is as the damaged culvert is believed to have caused poor water flow, subsequently leading to the soil nearby to give way. "The damaged culvert has caused underground water movement, and the floor has become hollow.

items.title: ‘Bring the evidence’: Anwar dismisses claims of stolen money moved to Africa
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim today rubbished allegations that he had ordered a former officer to keep stolen money in Africa, calling on those behind the claims to produce evidence. Anwar said he would not entertain baseless allegations made in a social media video as he was busy with other matters. The Prime Minister was responding to a widely circulated 49-second social media video in which a woman named Sofia Rini Buyong made several allegations involving Anwar and a former officer, including claims of stolen money being moved to Africa and the officer possessing unexplained wealth worth “billions of ringgit”.

items.title: Anwar says Negeri Sembilan Undang dispute to be managed under state laws
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Negeri Sembilan royal dispute should be managed at the state level. “Negeri Sembilan is calm. Four ruling chiefs — Mubarak, Undang of Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Undang of Johol Datuk Muhammed Abdullah and Undang of Rembau Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin — signed a joint declaration on April 19 to depose Yang di‑Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, citing misconduct.

items.title: MEX crash: Wife of victim discharged, still recovering from injuries
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 23-year-old Bangladeshi woman who survived a crash that claimed her husband's life has been discharged from hospital but remains unable to walk without assistance. Grieving Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, who sustained a broken leg in the incident, was discharged at 5.30pm yesterday, her uncle Mohibul Hasan, 50, said. In the suspected drink-driving crash early yesterday on the Maju Expressway (MEX), Nafisa's husband, Muzahid Millad, 22, was killed.

items.title: Bus driver jailed, fined RM8,000 for dangerous driving
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: An express bus driver was sentenced to seven months' jail and fined RM8,000 by the Magistrate's Court here today after pleading guilty to dangerous driving last week. The court also imposed an additional five months' jail if the fine is not paid and disqualified him from holding a driving licence for five years from today. Deputy public prosecutor Muhamad Ehsan Nasarudin urged the court to impose a heavier sentence, including a minimum one-year jail term, citing the seriousness of the offence and risks to public safety.

items.title: Man ordered to undergo psychiatric evaluation after courtroom outburst
items.summary: Two mosque committee members pursued and apprehended him before the incident was reported to the Sentul police command centre. The charges were framed under Section 298 of the Penal Code which carries a maximum one year jail, a fine or both upon conviction; and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act which provides a maximum RM100 fine. The charge under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act carries a maximum RM5,000 fine or a jail term not more than two years and police supervision of between two and three years.

items.title: MetMalaysia: No tsunami threat after 5.2-magnitude earthquake in Indonesia’s North Maluku Sea
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A moderate earthquake measuring 5.2 on the Richter scale was detected in the North Maluku Sea, Indonesia, at 12.45pm today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department said in a statement the earthquake occurred 120km east of Bitung, Indonesia, at a depth of 10km. However, a preliminary assessment found there was no tsunami threat to Malaysia.

items.title: NSTP booth draws crowds with youth-focused activities at Karnival Jom Heboh
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The New Straits Times Press booth at Karnival Jom Heboh here emerged as a vibrant crowd-puller, offering a dynamic mix of educational, creative and entertainment-based activities tailored especially for youths and families. Anchoring the booth's appeal were a series of engaging competitions, including the "Pertandingan Wartawan Cilik", which gave children a hands-on introduction to the world of journalism, as well as an artificial intelligence-themed challenge that reflected NSTP's forward-looking approach. The booth also featured interactive attractions such as a photo booth experience, supported by Dengku Gaming Zone, alongside the "U Klik" photo booth, both of which saw steady queues throughout the event.

items.title: Minister: Shah Alam council staffer caught littering from official vehicle will face same punishment as all
items.summary: IPOH, April 24 — A Shah Alam City Council staff member seen throwing cigarette butts from a council vehicle in a viral social media video will face disciplinary action and be subjected to a Community Service Order, Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said. He said no party was above the law and action would be taken against those committing littering offences in public places. The media reported yesterday that the Shah Alam City Council had referred the case to its Integrity Division in accordance with the Shah Alam City Council Officers (Conduct & Discipline) Rules 2023.

items.title: Trump seeks 'lasting deal' with Iran as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended
items.summary: DUBAI: Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire for three weeks at a meeting at the White House brokered by President Donald Trump, who said he was prepared to wait for "the best deal" to end his conflict with Iran. A day after Iran flaunted its tightened grip over the key shipping corridor, Trump dismissed the threat posed by Iran's "little wise-guy ships" and said he believed Teheran was hamstrung from making a deal because its leadership was in turmoil. On Thursday, he said the US could knock out in a day any refurbishing of weapons that Iran may have made during a ceasefire in place since April 8.

items.title: Missing Australian hiker found safe on Mount Santubong after overnight rescue operation
items.summary: KUCHING, April 24 — An Australian woman reported missing while hiking Mount Santubong yesterday was found safe at 11.59am today after a search and rescue operation launched last night. “On the second day, the SAR operation resumed this morning and the operation commander informed that the victim’s location had been identified in the F7 area (of the SAR operation) at 10.30am. The rescue team managed to communicate by calling out the victim’s name,” it said in a statement today.

items.title: Negri Sembilan royal dispute to be settled under state law, says PM
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has left the ongoing conflict between Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir and the four Undang to be resolved in accordance with state law. "The (crisis in) Negri Sembilan is calm now. Let it be managed under the Negri Sembilan law first.

items.title: Haze hits 10 Bachok schools as peat fires push API to very unhealthy levels
items.summary: BACHOK: Ten schools here have been affected by haze following ongoing peatland fires in Kampung Kuau and surrounding areas, with Air Pollutant Index (API) readings reaching very unhealthy levels. The affected schools are Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Kandis, Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Kandis, SMK Kubang Telaga, SK Kubang Telaga, SK Beris Lalang, SK Bakong, SK Telaga Ara, SMK Agama Tok Bachok (SMKATB), Sekolah Menengah Ugama (A) Darul Iman and SK Seneng. District Disaster Management Committee chairman Mohd Zaki Yusoff said the Department of Environment (DoE) had deployed a mobile air quality monitoring station at SK Beris Lalang to monitor the impact zone within a 5km radius.

items.title: ‘Resolve it this week’, Nga tells Ipoh mayor over old town parking complex land issue
items.summary: IPOH, April 24 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has instructed Ipoh Mayor Zamakhshari Hanipah to resolve land issues linked to the proposed parking and commercial complex at Jalan Panglima this week so construction can proceed. Nga said the issue must be settled by the latest this week to enable the project under the private finance initiative to move forward. He said the project is an initiative by the Ipoh City Council and a private company investing RM15 million in its development, with completion expected within 15 months.

items.title: U Business launches ULTRA Business Reserve for enterprise priority connectivity
items.summary: U Business has introduced ULTRA Business Reserve, mobile plan to support enterprises with priority connectivity, seamless roaming and built-in security. STRONGER PERFORMANCE The plan's main feature is ULTRA5G Reserve, which provides always-on reserved network capacity. SEAMLESS CONNECTIVITY ACROSS BORDERS U Business has also emphasised improving connectivity for business travellers.

items.title: Fahmi on two-day working visit to Penang to engage communities
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil is on a two-day working visit to Penang starting today to engage with local communities through various programmes. Tomorrow, Fahmi will present contributions from the Tabung Kasih@Hawana fund to RTM Penang assistant engineer Razif Saidin at Jalan Bagan Belat, Pekan Baru, Penaga. – Bernama TAGS: Fahmi Fadzil, working visit, Penang, Tabung Kasih@HAWANA, NADI © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Female interpreter, two men cleared of conspiring to murder her husband
items.summary: Judge Norliza Othman acquitted Leong Eng Eng, 41, Lim Yon Hay, 40, and Mok Kum Loon, 41, after the court found that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case. The court also allowed an application to withdraw the same charge against another man, Yon Hay's brother Lim Yon Loon, who died last year from tuberculosis. In her ruling, Norliza said the prosecution failed to prove that all the accused had conspired to murder in the incident, with no evidence from any prosecution witness confirming their conspiracy either directly or circumstantially.

items.title: Police: RM50,000 debt believed to have led to Johor restaurant shooting that killed three
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Police believe at least six shots were fired in the restaurant shooting at Taman Kota Jaya on Sunday that left three people dead. Johor police chief Ab Rahaman Arsad said investigations at the scene found six bullet casings. He said the 71-year-old suspect is believed to have acted over a RM50,000 debt involving one of the victims, a 37-year-old Vietnamese woman.

items.title: King, Johor Regent receive PM Anwar in audience
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim received an audience with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today. The meeting, held earlier today, was shared by Tunku Ismail in a post on his official Facebook page. "His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia and His Royal Highness Tunku Ismail, the Regent of Johor, received in audience the Prime Minister of Malaysia, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today, April 24, 2026," the post said.

items.title: China calls US accusations of AI technology theft 'baseless'
items.summary: BEIJING: Beijing on Friday refuted accusations made by the White House that Chinese entities were involved in "industrial-scale" efforts to steal US artificial intelligence technology. Asked about the matter at a regular news conference in Beijing on Friday, Chinese foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun said: "The US claims are entirely baseless." China firmly opposes this," he said.

items.title: Man killed, wife and children injured after SUV skids into ravine near Cameron Highlands
items.summary: The Tapah Fire and Rescue Station received a distress call at 8.43pm regarding the incident, which involved a Perodua Aruz sport utility vehicle. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the SUV skidded before falling into a three‑metre‑deep ravine and landing in a shallow river. The rescue operation, which ended at 11.02pm, involved 14 firefighters assisted by the Ringlet Fire and Rescue Station, supported by four vehicles including two Fire Rescue Tenders, a Rapid Response Vehicle and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services unit.

items.title: PM approves RM300,000 allocation to rebuild Pulau Gaya bridge
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government has approved a special allocation of RM300,000 to rebuild a pedestrian bridge at Kampung Selamat Lok Urai, Pulau Gaya, Sabah, which collapsed on March 22, according to the Implementation Coordination Unit, Prime Minister's Department (ICU PMD). It said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim approved the allocation following the incident, which saw several residents fall into the sea while visiting relatives during Aidilfitri. It said the allocation will be fully utilised to rebuild a stronger and safer pedestrian bridge, replacing the old and dilapidated structure, which posed safety risks.

items.title: Unisem slips into the red in Q1 on margin pressure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Unisem (M) Bhd slipped into the red in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, mainly due to weaker gross profit margins amid higher production costs. The group posted a net loss of RM13.35 million, compared with a net profit of RM6 million in the same quarter last year, according to a filing with Bursa Malaysia. Revenue, however, rose 9.7 per cent to RM464.75 million from RM423.62 million, driven mainly by higher sales volumes across its key operating segments.

items.title: Calls for justice grow online after Bangladeshi gamer dies in crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Calls for justice have intensified online following the death of a Bangladeshi national in a crash on the Maju Expressway, with many users demanding accountability over the incident. His wife, Nafisa Tabassum Adiba, 23, sustained injuries and is receiving treatment at Hospital Kuala Lumpur. Another user, @NURNOBI-k6y5n, posted: "We want justice, Bangladeshi people."

items.title: Suhakam calls for end to indefinite detention of children
items.summary: The call was made in response to ongoing concerns over the indefinite detention of children, which is seen as inconsistent with the fundamental principles of children's rights, including legal certainty, proportionality, rehabilitation, and reintegration. From a children's rights perspective, any law governing children in conflict with the law must meet at least four minimum requirements. "First, children must know the legal consequences of the sentence imposed.

items.title: Man killed when SUV plunged into river was headed to visit grandfather's grave
items.summary: TAPAH: A man's wish to pay respects to his late grandfather remained unfulfilled when he was killed after the sport utility vehicle he was driving skidded and plunged into a river at Batu 22, Jalan Tapah-Cameron Highlands, last night. A cousin of the victim, Salabiah Saharudin, 59, said Zamri had earlier been on his way to Cameron Highlands to visit his grandfather's grave. "A relative told us he wanted to go and pay his respects to his grandfather who had passed away in Cameron Highlands.

items.title: Sarawak aims to elevate plastic surgery sector to South Korea’s standards, says Dr Sim
items.summary: KUCHING, April 24 — Sarawak aims to develop its plastic and aesthetic surgery sector to a level comparable with South Korea, while maintaining strict standards in training, patient safety and regulatory compliance, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. Everyone says the best plastic surgery is in South Korea, and we go there. “Plastic surgery is not a new field.

items.title: Individual questioned over fake diesel export allegation, says MCMC
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has recorded the statement of an individual suspected of spreading false claims that Malaysia supplied diesel fuel to the Philippines. The individual, accompanied by a lawyer, appeared at the MCMC headquarters today to assist investigations into the viral allegations. "The seizure was carried out under Section 248 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (CMA)," it said.

items.title: Military officer remanded in murder probe over alleged drunk driving crash that killed Bangladeshi man on MEX
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the remand order was issued by Magistrate Guftan Nor Asyikin Murlikamal following a police application under Section 302 of the Penal Code. “The remand application was made at 10.45am today at the Dang Wangi District Police Headquarters remand room and was allowed for five days from today until next Tuesday (April 28),” he said in a statement today. Mohd Zamzuri said a 22-year-old Bangladeshi male passenger died at the scene from severe head injuries.

items.title: Fatal collision in Kimanis leaves two dead, one hurt
items.summary: KIMANIS, April 24 — Two people were killed and another was injured in a collision involving two vehicles along Jalan Bukit Kimanis-Keningau, here, yesterday. Upon arrival, they immediately conducted an operation to extricate two victims trapped in the vehicles who were later pronounced dead by medical officers at the scene. “A 61-year-old Perodua Axis driver died while its 52-year-old woman passenger suffered injuries.

items.title: 114,000 new jobs expected from digital investments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The upcoming BICSI Southeast Asia Malaysia 2026 Conference and Exhibition is set to be a major catalyst in empowering the nation's technology and digital sectors, particularly in human resource management. Human Resources Ministry secretary-general Datuk Azman Mohd Yusof said the event, scheduled for July 2026, will further cement Malaysia's position as a regional leader in the digital infrastructure ecosystem. The pre-launch event brought together key stakeholders, including government agencies and industry players such as the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation, Malaysia Technology Development Corporation, Invest Selangor, Selangor Information Technology and Digital Economy Corporation, and Cyberview.

items.title: Grief spreads online after Bangladeshi YouTuber Muzahid Millad dies in crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Bangladeshi online gaming community is mourning the death of Muzahid Millad, a Bangladeshi tourist who was killed in a suspected drink-driving crash on the Maju Expressway (MEX) early yesterday. Muzahid's YouTube channel, Advance Gaming, has more than 777,000 subscribers. A fellow gaming YouTube account, "Gamer With Proverb", uploaded a video titled "End of Mujahid Bhai (Advanced Gaming)", expressing condolences and sadness over Muzahid's death.

items.title: Dapsy urges Kuching North City Commission to inspect manhole covers after near‑miss at Sungai Maong market
items.summary: KUCHING, April 24 — The Kuching North City Commission (DBKU) has been urged to immediately conduct a thorough inspection of manhole covers at Sungai Maong Wet Market to prevent any untoward incidents. “A routine visit to Sungai Maong Wet Market this morning (Friday) took a dangerous turn for me. Lam, who is Dr Yii’s special assistant, said the market underwent upgrading works carried out by DBKU last year.

items.title: Scott's marks Children's Day with 'AnakSiapaNi' health campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Scott's marked Children's Day 2026 with its #AnakSiapaNi campaign celebrating children's achievements while promoting health, immunity and holistic development through family engagement. Led by healthcare company Haleon, the campaign centres on "proud parenting moments" — everyday instances when children show confidence, resilience and curiosity — while encouraging conversations around nutrition and wellbeing. Haleon general manager for Malaysia, Singapore and Brunei, Neelesh Suryavanshee, said the initiative reflects efforts to support children's holistic development.

items.title: Missing 77-year-old found safe after overnight jungle search in Limbang
items.summary: LIMBANG, April 24 — A 77-year-old man who went missing while fishing near Kampung Staie Bukit Sagan here was found safe yesterday after a search and rescue (SAR) operation that lasted several hours. The victim, Bujang Nyanau, was located on the third search attempt about four kilometres from his longhouse by a SAR team from the Limbang Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) at around 3.40pm yesterday. When Bujang failed to return by the following morning, a missing person report was lodged at the Limbang District Police Headquarters (IPD Limbang).

items.title: DBKL shuts beauty salon, raids two snooker outlets in Cheras [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has shut down a beauty premises and seized equipment from two snooker outlets during a raid on unlicensed businesses in Taman Shamelin Perkasa, Cheras, on Wednesday. In a statement today, DBKL said four premises were inspected under Operasi Tindakan Khas Premis Berisiko (Pusat Kecantikan/Snuker) Siri 5/2026. "DBKL ordered the immediate closure of a beauty premises, seized various equipment from two entertainment outlets (snooker centres), and issued seven compound notices for multiple offences," it said.

items.title: Housing approvals slump 22pct in February, weighs on Leading Index
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The number of housing units approved fell 22.2 per cent in February, weighing on Malaysia's leading economic indicators even as overall growth signals remained modestly positive, the Statistics Department said. On a month-on-month basis, the LI edged down 0.3 per cent, following declines in real imports of semiconductors and the number of new companies registered, both of which fell 0.6 per cent. The department said the overall increase in the LI was largely supported by stronger real imports of other basic precious and non-ferrous metals, which rose 18.7 per cent, as well as an 8.4 per cent expansion in real money supply.

items.title: Anwar confirms discussion with King on new MACC chief appointment, announcement to follow
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that he will announce the name of the new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner at a later date. He said he held discussions with His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, regarding the matter this morning. “I have discussed the matter with Tuanku (Sultan Ibrahim) earlier and I will announce (the name) at a later date,” he said briefly to reporters after visiting the Permas Jaya Farmers’ Market near Johor Bahru here today.

items.title: Man rescued after getting trapped in mud near Butterworth jetty
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: Firefighters rescued a man who became trapped in mud near a fishermen's jetty beside a floating seafood restaurant at Ujong Batu, Bagan Dalam, here today. The Penang Fire and Rescue Department received a distress call at 11.40am and deployed a team from the Butterworth fire and rescue station to the scene. Firefighters, wearing full personal protective equipment (PPE), carried out a safety assessment before conducting the rescue operation using a stretcher.

items.title: Sarawak’s first Orang Ulu full minister Bo’ Tajang Laing dies at 100
items.summary: KUCHING, April 24 — Former Sarawak minister Datuk Seri Bo’ Tajang Laing has passed away at the age of 100. His passing was announced by daughter Lohong Tajang in a Facebook post late last night. Tajang’s grandnephew Senator Pele Peter Tinggom said Tajang was an important figure in Sarawak’s early political history after the formation of Malaysia.

items.title: Police link Bangsar shooting to gang rivalry and territorial disputes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Long-standing grudges and territorial disputes between organised crime groups are believed to be among the motives behind the shooting incident at Jalan Telawi 2, Bangsar. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the incident, which occurred about 3am on Wednesday, involved a confrontation between two groups before a shot was fired. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: In unlikely twist, rescued South Korean kidnapping victim turns out to be Interpol-wanted fugitive
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the 40-year-old victim was travelling alone from Genting Highlands to Kuala Lumpur when his vehicle was tailed and later intercepted by his kidnappers on April 17. The victim’s friend later transferred US$3 million to the suspects’ cryptocurrency account before the victim was rescued. “So this kidnapping victim is actually wanted by South Korean authorities,” Kumar said, adding that victim was wanted for illegal gambling activities.

items.title: Kota Kinabalu man gets 15 months in jail for car theft disguised as purchase
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 24 — A 25-year-old man was sentenced to 15 months’ jail and fined RM2,000 or three months’ jail by the Magistrate’s Court here for stealing a car under the pretext of buying it. Aedan Dylan John pleaded guilty before Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus to stealing a Perodua Kenari EZ (Auto) from one Soong Che Wai, at about 1.30pm on March 30, this year, at a taxi parking area of the Kota Kinabalu International Airport. Police arrested Aedan on April 3, at 4.30pm in Sipitang.

items.title: Selangor records 209 water theft cases last year, most involving commercial accounts
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 24 — A total of 209 water theft cases were reported in Selangor last year, with most involving commercial accounts, the State Legislative Assembly was told today. State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said 184 of the cases involved commercial accounts, while the remaining 25 involved domestic accounts. He said 43 water theft cases had been recorded as of March this year, involving 12 domestic accounts and 31 commercial accounts.

items.title: Police detain foreigner in Niah after threat to kill sister, seize machete and homemade shotgun
items.summary: MIRI, April 24 — Police have arrested a 37-year-old foreign man for allegedly threatening to kill his younger sister with a machete in their home. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) received a report at 1.44pm on April 22 from the 35-year-old sister. Mohd Farhan said the suspect has been remanded for four days until April 27 to assist in investigations.

items.title: Penang CM urges cloud seeding as critically low Sungai Muda levels threaten state water supply
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang state government hopes cloud seeding operations can be carried out in upstream Kedah to raise water levels in Sungai Muda. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the measure was important to ensure sufficient raw water supply in the state. Chow said the Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) had contacted authorities in Kedah to coordinate immediate action.

items.title: Police arrest three teenagers in Tawau over alleged rape of 15-year-old schoolgirl
items.summary: District Police Chief ACP Jasmin Hussin said the suspects, including the main suspect, were detained at about 6.50pm on Wednesday at the Merotai police outpost. “Police moved swiftly to locate the suspects following the report,” he said yesterday. Police also detained two other suspects believed to have been involved in the incident.

items.title: Bukit Aman CID: Bangsar’s Jalan Telawi shooting tied to revenge over past gang turf disputes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — A shooting along Jalan Telawi in Bangsar on April 22 is believed to have stemmed from revenge linked to past territorial disputes between organised crime groups, police have confirmed. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said a 41-year-old local man was shot in the abdomen following an altercation between two groups outside an entertainment outlet during the incident. The victim’s condition is stable, Kumar said, adding that the two suspects arrested over the incident are currently in remand.

items.title: High Court sets May 14 for decision on Zahid’s bid for acquittal in Akalbudi case
items.summary: High Court Judge Nurulhuda Nur’aini Mohamad Nor fixed the date after counsel Datuk Hamidi Mohd Noh, representing Ahmad Zahid, informed the court that the defence had applied to vacate today’s decision date pending the Court of Appeal’s ruling on May 7. “The court sets May 14 for the decision, subject to the outcome of the Court of Appeal on May 7,” she said. Earlier, Hamidi said that on April 20, the defence had filed a letter seeking to vacate today’s decision date pending the appellate court’s ruling.

items.title: Selangor to adopt Year One entry at age six from 2027, hire teachers for influx
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 24 — The Selangor state government will adopt the Education Ministry's Year One pupil admission policy starting at age six for the 2027 school session, including primary schools handled by the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (JAIS). Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari informed the state assembly today that plans are in place to hire more teachers and upgrade school facilities to meet the influx of Year One students. “Secondly, coordinating student data to pinpoint densely populated areas alongside the Selangor State Education Department.

items.title: Penang LRT Mutiara Line construction on track, says CM amid political interference claims
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — The Penang LRT Mutiara Line project remains on schedule despite the tender process for Package Two still pending finalisation. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said works on Package One are ongoing, dismissing claims that political interference had caused delays. Asked about reports of political interference in the tender process for Package Two, Chow Kon Yeow said the state government was not involved, as the process is handled by project owner MRT Corp.

items.title: Penang cements role as Malaysia’s automation, test and equipment hub with launch of collaborative campus initiative
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 24 — Penang is strengthening its position as a key hub for automation, test and equipment (ATE) within Malaysia’s semiconductor industry with the launch of a new industry-led ATE campus. Penang chief minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state has the highest concentration of ATE companies and ecosystem partners in the country. “Within this ecosystem, Penang hosts more than 20 local ATE companies and over 80 automation, precision engineering, and component firms,” he said in his opening address at the launch of the ATE Campus in Olive Tree Hotel here.
